Let's say you're at a you're at an eight on the rapport scale eight being pretty good rapport Ten being the highest and you start moving them down to straight line. You're at eight. Does it stay here? No, it pours It dips up and down Constantly based on the last thing you say Based on the last tonality you used based on the last body language move or application that hit this person and They're rapport their sense of who you are. Do they feel that you care about them or Are you just there to make a commission? So watch imagine on one side of the equation a 10 would represent this person really really cares about me They have my best interest at heart all they want to do is they want to help me solve my problems That's a 10 report, right a one rapport be this person couldn't care less about me All they want to do is make a commission whether the product is gonna help me or not I can't trust this person as far as I can throw them. They don't care about me even one bit. That's a one Now in most cases you're not gonna really ever be so much at one or the other you're in the middle somewhere, right? Well, the idea is to stay close to a 10 as possible on that rapport scale But every time you speak They're judging you they're listening for cues both consciously and unconsciously and based on those conscious and unconscious cues they're gonna go up and down They're not gonna stay constant. Now When we get to the looping section of this of of First certification bother, if you're not on certification You gotta get on it. You're crazy, right? But when we get there Okay, I go through exactly how this word by word, but let me just give you the basics here. Remember this, you know Body language is an instrumental aspect of pacing and leading How you enter someone's world where they are and you pace them And you pace them And you lead them in the direction that you want to go meaning That you can affect someone else's body language. You can actually affect their their the way they feel about their their level of certainty on an emotional level by using Pacing and leading so in other words when I meet somebody, okay, I'm gonna basically adopt I enter their world where they are I want to and by the end part of this just so you know it differs Based on if you could be in a different part of the world You could be in the deep south you could be in in the new york city very different how people act their mannerisms But the whole idea with pacing and leading Is you enter a prospects world where they are in other words The biggest miss the biggest lie Is opposites attract they don't opposites don't attract we attract people Based on our similarities things that we have in common not the other way around You dramatically tip the odds in your favor of getting into rapport When you enter a prospects world where they are which is exactly why things like excessive jewelry excessive amounts of perfume overly long hair or you know wild hairdos Clothing that's striking that doesn't seem like it's normal. That's what it's really it's all about that At the highest level what it really chunks up to is it stops It impedes your ability to pace and lead Because you're entering someone's world where they're not And certainly not what they expected you to enter it was essentially it's it cuts off your ability to Move someone From one state to another from one body language moves to another They get because you you have to it's always about entering someone's world where they are just remember that Whether you're in person or over the phone I want you to know in in the body language module is all these exercises I'm crazy with the exercises. Why is it an active? It's an active module. You have to actually you're reading you're learning you're doing you're changing you're it's happening on this very workshop style, right? Please Don't just say don't just giggle. Oh, yeah, I know but please take this one to heart If you follow all the rules of body language I'm not saying you're gonna close people because of it What I'm saying is you set yourself up to have such an easy time of it Once you're perceived the right way Once people yeah, once they get that initial that gut reaction This is someone I want to do business with someone that's an expert. It makes everything else infinitely easier And without that by the way You know, you're in this sort of spot where you're playing catch up ball You just are now We're gonna go to the we're gonna do the q&a shortly here. And I just want to make sure that we're crystal clear here and that I'm not telling you to ever make things up To put phony pictures in your wallet put on a wedding band if you're not married So people will trust you because I think that well since he's in a trusting relationship. We must be trusted I'm not I never I never want you to do. I made that very clear in certification, right? But here's the thing. All right, what I'm telling you to do is always Put out the best version of you That's what this is about the best version of you Show up as your best self dress the right way groom yourself the right way put the appropriate amount of jewelry on and remember there's there's no clearly right answer or wrong answer one of the examples I use in certifications If you know if you're working in a holly davidson shop, you know, let's say you're selling motorcycles So, you know, who do you want a guy in a suit and tie? Well, we're not at scripting yet.
